author: Jason C.
pros: It is easy to navigate and a flexible query bar, you can also query using a single line
cons: it only has a max of 100 rows per page when querying

review_title: Studio 3T - Time saving and effective tool for noSQL to SQL
description: We have data in documentDB today and Studio 3T makes it easy to convert to SQL so our end users can use it.
author: Neha F.
pros: Easy to use with little to no technical knowledge require. Migration is easy with flexibility in languages. Visual query builder is a great tool that simplifies the complexity of noSQL data. Scheduler is great for running it off of own laptops. Import Export option is great.
cons: Task migration is tough if you switch laptops, also a dedicated server would be nice to run on the server instead of running on the laptop which has to be always ON.

description: it is a good product, but it can have improvements
author: Adrian F.
pros: It was easy to set up and start with it
cons: It changed the visual, time ago and it makes more difficult to make searchs

description: Nothing but positive. The additional tools and features go beyond free tools for the same purpose.
author: Shane K.
pros: Visual Query Builder and Aggregation Pipeline tools within Studio3T are by far my favorite. It allows me to sample different queries before every writing the implementation code in the respective project I am working on. The aggregation tools are fantastic allowing you to step through the different pipeline stages previewing the input outputs each step of the way.
cons: Hardly a con but it is definitely a little more pricey than something like Mongo Compass. But if the budget can swing it, it’s definitely worth the price.

review_title: An Escential tool for developers using MongoDB
description: An essential tool that every member of our team uses every day. I've tried all the free tools and there is really no comparison. Studio 3t saves money in time and productivity.
author: Robert S.
pros: The aggregation builder, the CSV export, Multi-document update.
cons: It was a bit expensive. There is no ability to increase the font size in text fields such as criteria, sorts, etc..

description: I think it's the best tool out there as of 2022 for managing data in MongoDB.
author: Aaron C.
pros: Aggregation builder is intuitive. It helps to be able to run through a long aggregation and pause at specific stages to see what the input will be to the next stage. Software is updated frequently and there is clearly active development going on.
cons: It's a memory hog! Segfaults are not out of the ordinary and it can be incredibly frustrating to lose where you are at in your work.

description: For MongoDB, no need to search for another software. Studio 3T is amazing!
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I've been using this since 2017. Compared to other products, query builder feature, detailed graphics and software GUI are very successful. Many years ago, as a student, when I wanted to use this application, they gave me a one-year free membership within a few hours. Since that day, I have been using studio 3t in my professional business life.
cons: In older versions, the software would crash occasionally. There were also connection problems. But thanks to the feedbacks, most of them have been fixed.

description: Studio 3T has been a smooth and seamless experience. Studio 3T has exceeded my expectations in terms of performance. No problems or difficulties with Studio 3T so far.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The Ultimate MongoDB Client for Developers and Data Analysts. The Key to Efficient and Effective MongoDB Management. The Future of MongoDB Management is Here with Studio 3T. The Most User-Friendly MongoDB Client on the Market
cons: Offers advanced features for data analysis and visualization. Supports all MongoDB versions and data types. Offers a 30-day trial period to evaluate the tool. Provides detailed documentation and support resources.

description: Studio 3T is sufficient for my usage, however a more IDE-like IntelliShell would be a great enhancement.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Studio 3T provides a lot of conveniences that I haven't seen from other MongoDB clients. The ability to interactively create indexes, aggregation pipelines, and view your data in various formats increases my productivity and I can see it being a good way to learn about Mongo.
cons: Studio 3T's IntelliShell feature could be more customizable and consistent. It is a bit cumbersome to use compared to modern IDE's.

review_title: While the cost of a license is high the functionality of 3T is unbeatable
description: In my experience with 3T I have found it to be a superfluid way of managing the multiple domains that we at our company host. Studio 3T makes it easy to bounce data around between sites when necessary while also providing you with a fundamentally sound way of hosting a local site with multiple datasets for a personal instance. I would recommend it to anyone looking to step up their DBMS game.
author: Sean D.
pros: Accepts multiple languages Has a build-in visual query builder for ease of use Made database management easier and fluid for my team Incredible for managing multiple domains
cons: The price of the license is high which means not everyone on my team is able to have access. This doesn't seem to be much of a hindrance however as many team members don't have the skillset needed for this to be useful to them.

author: Irina L.
pros: I liked the most that there is an option for us who don't know to write Mongo queries to get the data with Query Builder! And there are tutorials
cons: When I switched license type with still having the license it has let me do that even though my email was in the system
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Ricardo G.
pros: Ease of use. Price everything was what my company was looking for.
cons: Nothing. all the features that we need it were there.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Easy-to-use advanced client for MongoDB databases with SQL translation
description: Studio 3T is a pretty complete solution for a company that relies in MongoDB databases and multi functional teams. Migrations its really a topic that Studio handles well and make your life easier when need to run batches of data at once. The Schema analysis also its useful to help clean up and normalize the NoSQL database, which sometimes has old properties on it.
author: Thiago V.
pros: Studio 3T has a built in shell solution called "Intelishell", which makes out of the box to create JS scripts to run directly in the DB with a good performance. Also, the easy translation to SQL help other team members that isn't familiar with NoSQL language being universal used. Other thing I would highlight its the Explain tab on the query: specially for companies that has sharded collections, it helps out explain how much time its taking for each shard to gather the result of the query. This is really useful in our production daily logic and performance improvements.
cons: I would say that the UI isn't the best appealing and has too many tabs and modals that pop up in my opinion. Sometimes I just wanna a way to easier manage all the tabs I have opened (and maybe save them if have a crash). Also I think leak in integration with other products or even maybe with MongoDB Atlas (would be useful to expand the setup on Atlas directly from the Studio 3T UI).

review_title: Essencial and the best tool for Database exports
description: Positive! I use it frequently with a constant level of success.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: In the company we use it to generate reports and easily export versioning information so our CSM can pass this information onto customers.
cons: The exporting of large datasets can be very slow and often crash. It's a great tool but I run into this issue frequently.

description: I have been using Studio 3T to manage MongoDB databases for almost a year. I perform various operations on these databases.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Studio 3T has all the necessary functionality to work with NoSQL databases. You can easily query databases and manipulate data. I appreciate the management of connections to different servers.
cons: It is very advanced and it is easy to get lost in the extensive UI.

author: Lanny Z.
pros: The UI design is user friendly. It did not take too much time for me to get started with Studio 3T when I start develop my application with Mongo. It is also very helpful to manage the Atlas instance with Studio 3T. I can clearly manage all of my local and cloud instance in 3T
cons: The customized query is not as easy as the GUI query. I have to open an console in 3T and type my own command. It would be better if 3T can have auto-complete and helpful messages (like sample) when I type my command.

review_title: Easy to use for novice users or those coming from SQL
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I loved how easy it was to transition to using Mongo with Studio 3T. As a somewhat novice user of database querying, I love the option to use the visual query builder. The support documentation and training available was also a wonderful option.
cons: The one thing I'd love to see is the ability to group (and name the group) tabs for collections - to minimize and expand those tabs depending on what I'm working.

review_title: Best tool for document based databases
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I like the options for viewing the data: Table, Tree, and JSON view. It enables us to connect to MongoDB Atlas with is very helpful. Unlike Compass it is very simple and easy to use.
cons: It is a little tricky to connect to databases at first but then all is great after that.

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Tree to Table view is very convenient when looking through multiple records.
cons: When trying to Export data it is a little tedious to get it into a spreadsheet.

review_title: Studio 3T Fabulous Tool of the Decade
author: Srikanth J.
pros: user friendly and also not expensive tool . Easy to Learn and implement applications using this tool.
cons: IT would be nice if the export feature have further more formats to export. Also if there is free version similar to compass db limiting to 1GB so helpful for beginners and students.

review_title: Easy transition from SQl to NoSQL database
description: Great software from variety of similar products . Based on users feedbacks that is very important
author: Yuri E.
pros: I do not need to know NoSQL query syntax, it has automatic conversion from SQL to NoSQL
cons: Some of features are not trivial and should spend a sufficient training

description: Great experience overall, maybe I'm being too nit picky but sometimes things crash
author: Zach R.
pros: Generally stable, good UI, feature rich offering
cons: Expensive, sometimes migrations crash and you have to use another tool

description: I like this software a lot and no other free software propose these features.
author: Daniel H.
pros: Manage your mongo databases very easily. Build aggregations easily and reuse your scripts with the save feature. Export your data easily and import it elsewhere (even single documents from one db to another db). Dumping easily
cons: The user experience should be improved in some screens

description: Great appliation to work with MongoDB. It's really easy, especially if you don't work with MongoDB on a daily basis.
author: Fabian E.
pros: This application was made for MongoDB. All the views and actions are aligned to work with MongoDB. Other products can handle MongoDB too, but they still behave like SQL tools. The visual query builder is a great help for quickly filtering your data. The aggregation view gives the required structure to build larger pipelines.
cons: It's not distributed via package manager on linux platforms. The date type in the query builder could provider a date picket widget, so I don't have to edit the dates manually.

review_title: Great product to work with mongodb
description: Very good, easy to use tool.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Easily interacting with collections and seamlessly updating/adding property values.
cons: Building complex aggregation queries is difficult, the tool could improve with a helper to facilitate the process.
